user_query:
I'm getting our tailgate materials ready and I want a small bilingual fact sheet to needle Cowboys fans using real game stats. Please work entirely from my local files under input/ and produce two outputs:

1) output/taunt_cards.csv
2) output/selection_report.md

Here’s what I need, with deterministic rules so anyone can re-run it and get the same result:

Inputs to use:
- Games data: input/games.csv
- Templates directory: all *.json files under input/templates/
- Stop phrases: input/stop_phrases.txt

Task:
1) Inspect the input/templates/ directory to find every JSON template file and read them all. Merge templates by their "id" field. If the same id appears in multiple files, keep the version from the file whose name ends with the higher vN number (e.g., taunt_templates_v2.json overrides v1 for the same id). All merged templates must be considered for assignment.
2) Remove any template whose English text contains any stop phrase from input/stop_phrases.txt (case-insensitive match on whole words).
3) From input/games.csv, extract structured game records and compute:
   - winner, loser, scoreline (e.g., "Eagles 44–6" or "Eagles 33–10"), and point margin (winner score minus loser score).
   - Only keep games from season >= 2000 where the winner is the Philadelphia Eagles.
4) Rank the remaining games by margin (descending). Break ties by date (more recent first, descending). Select the top 5.
5) Assign templates to the 5 selected games deterministically: sort the remaining templates by id ascending and apply them in round-robin order to the ranked games (cycle through the sorted list as needed). Fill placeholders in each template using these fields when present: {season}, {week}, {winner}, {loser}, {scoreline}, {margin}, {venue}. If a placeholder is unused in a template, that’s okay.
6) Produce both an English and a Spanish version of each taunt:
   - Keep team names ("Eagles" and "Cowboys") and the exact scoreline string in English, unchanged.
   - Translate all other parts of the message to Spanish in a natural, fan-friendly tone without adding profanity or any stop phrase.
7) Write output/taunt_cards.csv with these columns for each of the 5 games, in ranked order: rank, date, season, week, venue, winner, loser, scoreline, margin, template_id, english_text, spanish_text.
8) Write output/selection_report.md that summarizes:
   - Total games parsed; count after filtering to Eagles wins since 2000; the 5 selected games with date and margin in order.
   - All template files discovered under input/templates/.
   - Number of templates loaded before and after stop-phrase filtering; list of template ids dropped and which stop phrase triggered each drop.
   - The final template assignment mapping (rank -> template_id) and the tie-break rule applied.

Notes:
- Do not randomize anything. Follow the tie-break and template assignment rules exactly.
- Preserve punctuation and digits in scorelines as-is; translate only surrounding words.
- Team names must remain exactly as in the data ("Eagles", "Cowboys"). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
